why_good: First non-animal, non-character subject. First use of collage medium (cut paper on construction paper). Renders a Mayan stepped pyramid (Tomb of Pakal) with careful architectural symmetry and educational-style labeling with arrows. Demonstrates medium flexibility (drawing to collage), subject flexibility (characters to architecture), and integration of academic content (first-grade ancient civilizations curriculum) into art.
transferable_pattern: Medium and subject flexibility: switching from familiar drawing to collage construction, and from narrative/character subjects to architectural/historical content, while maintaining precision and intentional labeling. The educational arrow-labeling style shows awareness of diagrammatic conventions distinct from narrative art.
tradeoffs: School-directed project — less spontaneous than self-initiated work. The flexibility score (5/5) is high because it represents the widest departure from prior work in medium, subject, and labeling convention.
review_criteria: Observed artifact (evidence tier 4). School collage with child commentary confirming source (learned about Pakal in school). Flexibility score 5/5.
